sae_features.label: Sliding clamps and enolpyruvyltransferases
sae_features.description: DNA polymerase sliding clamps and clamp-related checkpoint/processivity subunits (β-clamp/PCNA and the 9‑1‑1 clamp), with secondary cross‑activation on enolpyruvyltransferase family enzymes (AroA/MurA) around their substrate‑binding/active‑site loops

sae_features.label: DNA polymerase sliding clamp detector
sae_features.description: Detector of DNA polymerase processivity clamps—PCNA/β-clamp/9-1-1 and related viral processivity factors—keying on the conserved clamp-domain fold, especially repeated loop→β-strand elements within each clamp domain; it cross-activates weakly on short secondary-structure transition segments that mimic these motifs in unrelated proteins

sae_features.label: Exposed β-hairpin interaction surfaces
sae_features.description: Structural preference for solvent-exposed β-strand–turn elements and adjacent flexible terminal/loop segments within α/β proteins, which often form interaction or regulatory surfaces (e.g., in ring assemblies and small-molecule regulatory domains), rather than catalytic cores

sae_features.label: Hydrophobic membrane helices and cores
sae_features.description: Hydrophobic, ordered structural segments: the feature highlights long, contiguous hydrophobic stretches that form stable secondary-structure elements—most prominently transmembrane helices of secretory/endomembrane transporters—and also the hydrophobic cores and β/α elements of compact soluble domains (e.g., AP2/ERF DNA-binding and C2 lipid-binding modules), consistent with broad activation across structured regions in diverse proteins.

sae_features.label: Sliding clamp fold and helices
sae_features.description: DNA replication sliding-clamp ring fold and its partner-interaction surfaces: the feature primarily captures the PCNA/β-clamp/9-1-1 toroidal clamp architecture and secondarily short amphipathic helix–loop elements that mimic or contact clamp-binding grooves.

sae_features.label: C-terminal effector modules
sae_features.description: C-terminal functional regions that serve as terminal interaction/effector modules—typically the last domain or tail of a protein, often intrinsically disordered activation/sorting segments in regulators and secretory proteins, but also the final repeat/domain in multi-repeat scaffolds.

sae_features.label: Surface-exposed interaction/gating patches
sae_features.description: Surface-exposed interaction/gating patches: long, contiguous helix–loop–strand or TM–loop segments within compact domains that form binding interfaces (protein, lipid, nucleotide, or DNA), with a compositional bias toward acidic/polar residues (D/E/G/T) and, in extracellular cysteine-rich folds, cysteines and N‑glycosylation sites.

sae_features.label: Solvent-exposed beta-loop signature
sae_features.description: A cross-kingdom feature marking solvent-exposed beta-strand/loop segments within repeated, beta-rich binding/scaffold domains and processivity modules, recurring in nucleic-acid–binding S1/OB-fold proteins (rRNA biogenesis), DNA-binding plant factors (AP2/ERF, B3), replication clamps, extracellular adhesion modules (Ig/FN3/Laminin-G) and Ly6/uPAR three-finger proteins, as well as cupredoxin/cupin enzyme scaffolds; also present in nuclear transcriptional regulators.
